verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Outrages of the Arians against the Bishops.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: For whom have they ever persecuted and taken, that they have not insulted and injured as they pleased?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Whom have they ever sought after and found, that they have not handled in such a manner, that either he has died a miserable death, or has been ill-treated in every way?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Whatever the magistrates appear to do, it is their work; and the others are merely the tools of their will and wickedness.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: In consequence, where is there a place that has not some memorial of their malice?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Who has ever opposed them, without their conspiring against him, inventing pretexts for his ruin after the manner of Jezebel?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Where is there a Church that is not at this moment lamenting the success of their plots against her Bishops?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Antioch is mourning for the orthodox Confessor Eustathius; Balaneæ for the most admirable Euphration; Paltus and Antaradus for Kymatius and Carterius; Adrianople for that lover of Christ, Eutropius, and his successor Lucius, who was often loaded with chains by their means, and so perished; Ancyra mourns for Marcellus, Berrhœa for Cyrus, Gaza for Asclepas.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Of all these, after inflicting many outrages, they by their intrigues procured the banishment; but for Theodulus and Olympius, Bishops of Thrace, and for us and our Presbyters, they caused diligent search to be made, to the intent that if we were discovered we should suffer capital punishment: and probably we should have so perished, had we not fled at that very time contrary to their intentions.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: For letters to that effect were delivered to the Proconsul Donatus against Olympius and his fellows, and to Philagrius against me.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: And having raised a persecution against Paul, Bishop of Constantinople, as soon as they found him, they caused him to be openly strangled at a place called Cucusus in Cappadocia, employing as their executioner for the purpose Philip, who was Prefect.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: He was a patron of their heresy, and the tool of their wicked designs.
